hypothesis: Incorporating SEM images from intermediate cycling states (e.g., 25, 50, and 75 cycles) as auxiliary training data in the source domain pretraining stage will disproportionately improve the transfer learning framework's ability to classify formation-aged and 100-cycle target domain samples, because the model will learn a continuous morphological degradation trajectory—capturing gradual crack initiation and particle boundary evolution—that provides richer transferable representations than the discrete three-state (pristine, formation, 100-cycle) pretraining currently used, and this improvement will be quantifiable as a statistically significant increase in F1 scores specifically for aged classes while leaving pristine class performance unchanged.
